methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Skyeton is a growth-stage company in the Defense & Security sector founded by Roman Knyazhenko. The valuation universe for comparable analysis spans 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set exhibits significant fragmentation by stage and valuation tier.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow filters. Of 21 same-sector peers identified, only zero companies shared Skyeton's stage, one fell into an adjacent stage, and zero aligned on valuation tier. This shortfall prevents statistically robust median triangulation without risking cross-stage or cross-tier contamination. A broad all-sector median approach would obscure meaningful Defense & Security dynamics and is therefore not warranted.

Given the data constraints, this analysis cannot establish a defensible valuation range through traditional comparable-company methods. A credible valuation of Skyeton would require either: (1) acquisition of financial data (revenue, EBITDA, growth multiples) from the identified private peers to enable revenue or earnings multiple approaches; (2) expansion of the comp universe to include earlier or later-stage Defense & Security transactions; or (3) reliance on alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis with disclosed financial projections. The fragmented peer landscape underscores a broader challenge in valuing growth-stage frontier-tech defense companies, where heterogeneity in business models and maturity limits conventional benchmarking.
